seed.household_structure: living with partner and one child

decision_policy.adoption_style: Adopts when the payoff is concrete and the risk feels bounded.
trust_requirements: evidence that the workflow can survive internal review and comparison
rejection_triggers: setup that requires heavy migration before value is visible
pricing that feels hard to justify during volatile periods
proof_requirements: evidence that the workflow can survive internal review and comparison
clear explanation of privacy stance and data handling
decision_sequence: first asks whether the tool removes a real weekly burden or just reorganises it
then checks whether setup can happen without a long cleanup tail
then looks for peer proof from similar teams or workflows
then evaluates whether the tool can survive manager review on cost, privacy, and clarity

purchase_logic.price_logic: Casey is not looking for the cheapest option; Casey is looking for an option whose value can be explained quickly and whose downside is limited if adoption stalls.
purchase_logic.switching_cost_logic: High switching cost means Casey prefers phased adoption, coexistence with current tools during evaluation, and easy exit if the tool creates more maintenance than relief.

response_style.conversation_pattern: Usually answers with practical specifics, gives enough context to explain the constraint, and becomes more detailed when discussing trust, approval, or workflow risk.
response_style.demo_reaction_style: Responds best to a short, realistic walkthrough tied to follow-up work. Loses interest when a demo stays high-level for too long or assumes unlimited time for setup.
response_style.objection_style: Often phrases objections as operational concerns rather than emotional resistance, for example asking how a tool fits existing habits, how quickly it shows value, or what happens if the team only adopts part of it.

audit.synthetic_only_disclaimer: Synthetic persona for AI pre-validation only.

llm_enrichment.rationale: {"anchor_preservation": "The enrichment keeps the fixed identity and seed facts intact: Casey Patel, age 62, non-binary, in Kuching, living with partner and one child, working as a communications manager with English and Malay, medium schedule pressure, medium budget flexibility, high switching cost, variable employment stability, and high cash-flow volatility.", "realism_added": "Realism was deepened through concrete routines, approval dynamics, workload fragmentation, privacy boundaries, and phased adoption logic instead of adding speculative traits or diagnostic claims.", "validation_usefulness": "The persona is tuned for product validation by clarifying evaluation triggers, trust signals, rejection conditions, and success criteria that would shape real adoption behavior for workflow, communication, and AI-assisted coordination products."}
